answer:
**Yājñavalkya Smṛti chapter 1 frames the authority of law (*dharma*) through an explicit enumeration of its traditional sources and sages, rather than a cosmological creation narrative.**[^1]

It opens with sages honoring Yājñavalkya and requesting a full exposition of *dharma* for the *varṇas* (social orders), life-stages, and related matters.[^2] Yājñavalkya responds by directing them to learn *dharma* in the land of the black antelope (a ritually pure region) and immediately lists **fourteen sources of knowledge and *dharma***: the Vedas, Aṅgas (auxiliary sciences), Dharmaśāstra, Mīmāṃsā, Nyāya, and Purāṇas.[^3]

He then names **twenty sages as promulgators of Dharmaśāstras**, including Manu, Viṣṇu, himself (Yājñavalkya), Uśanas, Āpastamba, Kātyāyana, and Vasiṣṭha.[^4] The chapter further defines the **roots of *dharma*** as: revelation (*śruti*, i.e., Vedas), tradition (*smṛti*), conduct of the virtuous, self-pleasing actions, and desire born of right resolve.[^5] The highest *dharma* is declared as the vision of the Self through yoga, with authority vested in Vedic experts or assemblies thereof.[^6]

In contrast, Manusmṛti chapter 1 begins similarly with sages approaching Manu but pivots to a mythic account of creation by the Self-existent One (Svayambhu), from whom Manu receives and transmits the *śāstra*.[^7]

[^1]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [ch. 1](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/).
[^2]: [*p-97f0a4*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-97f0a4).
[^3]: [*p-b83e2e*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-b83e2e).
[^4]: [*p-0f4ad0*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-0f4ad0), [*p-95ef53*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-95ef53).
[^5]: [*p-7d795b*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-7d795b).
[^6]: [*p-1e45c4*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-1e45c4), [*p-c7a1e8*](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-c7a1e8).
[^7]: Manusmṛti, [ch. 1](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/), esp. [*p-879544*](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-879544) to [*p-54fad8*](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/01/translation/#p-54fad8).
